title.zh: 科學前沿：蜜蜂認知能力、植物免疫策略與環境變遷
title.en: Scientific Frontiers: Bee Cognition, Plant Immunity, and Environmental Change

summary.zh: 【總結】本期刊涵蓋多領域突破性研究，包括蜜蜂展現出乎意料的認知能力能自發解決問題、植物免疫受體模仿敵人以對抗病原體、奈米材料在空間限制下的穩定性、遙距黑洞質量測量方法、病毒傳播模式、哺乳期腸道屏障強化、燃料電極改進、經濟發展與生物多樣性保護共存的可能性、自由基偶聯化學、關節軟骨保護、疫苗效益、全球合作行為、心臟細胞治療、溫室氣體利用、森林砍伐監測、氣候變遷下的物種適應、睡眠與記憶關聯、遠程工作心理健康、森林火災與臭氧污染、紅樹林恢復、熱電材料優化以及癌症標靶治療。這些研究展現了科學在解決複雜問題方面的進展。【研究問題】本研究探討的問題橫跨多個學科：蜜蜂是否具備高層次認知能力？植物免疫系統如何演化以對抗病原體？如何在原子尺度控制奈米材料結構？如何精確測量遙距黑洞質量？病毒如何長期存續並影響人類健康？哺乳期如何影響母體免疫？如何優化燃料電池性能？經濟發展與環境保護能否共存？自由基反應如何產生特定立體構型？關節炎的分子機制是什麼？疫苗對公共健康的潛在影響如何？全球合作行為的決定因素是什麼？肥胖相關心臟衰竭的亞型與治療策略？如何利用二氧化碳進行化學轉化？如何精確監測全球森林變化？物種間競爭如何影響氣候變遷適應？情緒記憶如何影響睡眠？遠程工作對心理健康的影響？森林火災如何影響臭氧污染？紅樹林恢復進展如何？如何優化熱電材料性能？針對膽管癌的有效治療標的是什麼？這些問題涉及基礎科學與應用科學的關鍵挑戰。【研究方法】研究人員採用了多樣化的方法：蜜蜂行為實驗測試其自發解決問題能力；結構分析與工程化設計植物免疫受體；在氮化硼奈米管內限制生長奈米材料；利用引力透鏡提高遙距黑洞的空間解析度；病毒序列分析與種群動力學建模；免疫細胞分化與腸道屏障功能研究；鉀蒸發技術改進電極性能；土地使用決策的跨國建模與優化；自由基偶聯化學的催化控制；軟骨細胞功能與Wnt信號路徑分析；疫苗效益的數值模擬；全球合作行為的大規模實驗；心臟細胞功能與肥胖關聯分析；二氧化碳在光催化反應中的應用；高解析度衛星影像分析物種適應實驗；腦部記憶痕跡映射；遠程工作模式的心理健康評估；臭氧濃度的高解析度數據集分析；紅樹林變化的衛星監測；熱電材料摻雜技術；單細胞RNA测序與抗體開發。【結果】關鍵發現包括：允許玩耍的蜜蜂能自發學習使用球體作為工具獲取獎賞；大麥受體MLA3模仿宿主蛋白結構以對抗真菌；在氮化硼奈管內成功製備直徑接近1奈米的二硫化鉬奈米管；利用引力透鏡精確測量遙距黑洞質量；Jamestown Canyon病毒在蚊子卵中越冬形成年度循環；哺乳期間腸道內嗜酸性粒細胞累積促進黏液生成；鉀蒸發技術顯著提升燃料電池性能與耐用性；幾乎所有國家都有可能同時實現生物多樣性保護、氣候緩解和經濟價值優化；自由基偶聭反應可產生特定立體構型產品；Tiki2蛋白質限制Wnt信號以維持軟骨穩定；HSV-1疫苗可減少18-57%口腔感染和20-69%生殖器感染；全球超過三分之二的人表現出合作行為但低估他人合作意願；肥胖相關心臟衰竭可分為兩個生物學亞型；二氧化碳可作為氧化劑切割烯烴；2018年超過80%的樹冠損失是由機械清除造成；競爭限制物種在分布區核心的適應但促進邊緣適應；負面記憶增加非快速眼動睡眠到覺醒的轉換；獨自居住的遠程工作者心理健康風險顯著增加；2015-2024年森林火災導致臭氧相關早逝增加46%；2010年以來全球紅樹林開始恢復；鉻摻雜鉛硒化物實現低功耗高冷卻性能；Claudin-1是膽管癌有前景的治療標靶。
summary.en:
**Summary:** This journal issue covers breakthrough research across multiple disciplines, including bees demonstrating unexpected cognitive abilities to spontaneously solve problems, plant immune receptors mimicking enemies to combat pathogens, stability of nanomaterials under spatial confinement, methods for measuring distant black hole masses, viral transmission patterns, gut barrier fortification during lactation, fuel cell cathode improvements, potential coexistence of economic development and biodiversity conservation, stereoselective radical coupling chemistry, cartilage protection mechanisms, vaccine benefits, global cooperation behavior, cardiomyocyte treatment strategies, CO2 utilization in chemical transformations, forest monitoring, species adaptation under climate change, emotion-sleep relationships, mental health impacts of remote work, wildfire-ozone pollution connections, mangrove recovery, thermoelectric optimization, and cancer target therapies. These studies demonstrate scientific progress in addressing complex challenges.

**Problem:** The research addresses critical questions across disciplines: Do bees possess high-level cognitive abilities? How do plant immune systems evolve against pathogens? How to control nanomaterial structures at atomic scales? How to precisely measure distant black hole masses? How do viruses persist long-term and affect human health? How does lactation affect maternal immunity? How to optimize fuel cell performance? Can economic development coexist with environmental protection? How to achieve stereoselective radical reactions? What are molecular mechanisms of arthritis? What are public health impacts of vaccines? What determines global cooperation behavior? What are subtypes and treatments for obesity-related heart failure? How to utilize CO2 in chemical transformations? How to monitor global forest changes? How does interspecific competition affect climate adaptation? How do emotional memories affect sleep? What are mental health impacts of remote work? How do wildfires affect ozone pollution? What is the status of mangrove recovery? How to optimize thermoelectric materials? What are effective targets for cholangiocarcinoma treatment?

**Approach:** Researchers employed diverse methodologies: bee behavior experiments testing spontaneous problem-solving; structural analysis and engineering of plant immune receptors; confined growth of nanomaterials within boron nitride nanotubes; gravitational lensing for distant black hole mass measurement; viral sequence analysis and phylodynamics modeling; immune cell differentiation and gut barrier function studies; potassium evaporation techniques for electrode improvement; cross-country modeling and optimization of land-use decisions; catalytic control of radical coupling reactions; cartilage cell function and Wnt signaling pathway analysis; numerical modeling of vaccine benefits; large-scale experiments on global cooperation; analysis of cardiomyocyte function-obesity relationships; CO2 utilization in photocatalytic reactions; high-resolution satellite monitoring of forest changes; experimental studies of species adaptation; brain engram mapping for memory-sleep relationships; mental health assessments of remote work patterns; high-resolution ozone datasets analyzing wildfire impacts; satellite monitoring of mangrove changes; chromium doping of thermoelectric materials; and single-cell RNA sequencing with antibody development for cancer targets.

**Results:** Key findings include: Bees allowed to play spontaneously learned to use balls as tools to access rewards; barley MLA3 receptor mimics host protein structure to combat fungi; successful synthesis of molybdenum disulfide nanotubes with diameters approaching 1 nm within boron nitride nanotubes; precise measurement of distant black hole mass using gravitational lensing; Jamestown Canyon virus overwinters in mosquito eggs forming annual cycles; eosinophil accumulation during lactation promotes mucus production; potassium evaporation significantly improves fuel cell performance and durability; nearly all countries show potential for simultaneous gains in biodiversity, climate mitigation, and economic value; radical coupling reactions can produce stereoselective products; Tiki2 protein restricts Wnt signaling to maintain cartilage stability; HSV-1 vaccine could reduce oral infections by 18-57% and genital infections by 20-69%; over two-thirds of people globally are cooperative but underestimate others' cooperation; obesity-related heart failure clusters into two biological subtypes; CO2 can serve as an oxidant to cleave alkenes; in 2018, over 80% of tree cover loss was caused by mechanical clearing; competition limits adaptation at species range cores but promotes it at edges; negative memories increase transitions from non-REM sleep to wakefulness; remote workers living alone show significantly increased mental health risks; wildfire emissions reversed ozone reduction trends from 2015-2024, increasing premature deaths by 46% after 2013; mangroves have been recovering globally since 2010, expanding into new habitats; ultralow chromium doping achieves low-power, high-cooling-density thermoelectric performance; Claudin-1 represents a promising therapeutic target for cholangiocarcinoma.
